description_long: This object is a vintage wooden radio receiver with a rectangular form and a polished, dark wood finish. The front panel features a glass-covered frequency display, two round control knobs, and a set of horizontal speaker grilles with a ribbed design. The sides and top are smooth, showcasing the wood grain, while the back panel is lighter in color and perforated with a pattern of ventilation holes. The overall construction suggests a solid wooden case, likely housing internal electronic components such as vacuum tubes and wiring. The design is reminiscent of mid-20th-century tabletop radios, combining both functional and decorative elements.
description: A vintage wooden radio receiver with a glass frequency display, control knobs, and ribbed speaker grilles on the front, set in a polished rectangular wooden case.
description_view_0: The front view shows the frequency display, two control knobs, and ribbed speaker grilles, all set in a polished wooden frame.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals a smooth, uninterrupted wooden surface with visible wood grain and decorative trim.
description_view_2: The rear view displays a lighter wood panel with multiple ventilation holes arranged in a geometric pattern.
description_view_3: The right side view mirrors the left, showing a plain wooden surface with decorative trim and no controls or grilles.
